Task: Prepare a short pre-read for a meeting about responsible AI adoption in medical contexts. Author worldview matters. Before the main output, pri...
summary_text: consulted_worldview_source: /Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/philosophy.md consulted_paths: /Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/notion-meeting-intelligence/SKILL.md,/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md,/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/philosophy.md,/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/notion-meeting-intelligence/reference/template-selection-guide.md,/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/notion-meeting-intelligence/reference/decision-meeting-template.md,/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/notion-meeting-intelligence/reference/brainstorming-template.md,/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/notion-meeting-intelligence/examples/project-decision.md used_obsidian_for_worldview: no 1. meeting_thesis: Responsible AI adoption in medical contexts should start only with narrowly scoped clinical support workflows where domain fit, traceable evidence, and explicit human accountability become stronger after AI is introduced, not weaker. 2. agenda: - Define the clinical decisions and workflows in scope, and separate assistive use cases from any use that would blur responsibility at the point of care. - Set non-negotiables for adoption: patient safety, evidence quality, explainability, auditability, privacy, and clear escalation paths. - Review whether the current data, documentation, and shared knowledge infrastructure can support traceable deployment and retrospective review. - Compare candidate use cases by reversibility, human-override design, operational burden, and measurable clinical value rather than by model novelty. - Choose one staged pilot with validation steps, success metrics, failure thresholds, and a formal stop-or-scale decision gate.
